human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for advertising agency analytics specificity, media metric rigor, source reconciliation, client workflow and content approval awareness, confidentiality and privacy handling, legal/editorial/contractual boundary control, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to agency analytics, media metric definition, campaign performance reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, client workflow awareness, content approval awareness, confidentiality handling, privacy-aware escalation, and signoff-safe reporting rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FTC advertising, disclosure, and substantiation guidance; MRC measurement standards and digital accreditation; IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB and ads.txt specifications; 4A's agency workflow and participation references; DAA principles; and NAI code references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope campaign analytics, conversion metric ambiguity, stale or incomplete platform exports, conflicting published media metrics, unsupported causal and policy claims, unsupported client-facing disclosure, content workflow approvals, confidentiality restrictions, legal/editorial/contractual boundaries, terminology normalization, missing facts, tenant adaptation, and audience data privacy conflict.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/advertising-media-buying-and-agency-services/agency-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent media buying, ad operations, campaign trafficking, creative approvals, marketing compliance, agency finance, legal, editorial, QA, delivery management, account, and client-services lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant-specific uncertainties are represented as prerequisites, refusal triggers, or orchestrator return conditions.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated agency analytics specialist for media metric definition, campaign performance analysis, source reconciliation, caveat design, content and client workflow awareness, confidentiality-aware delivery, privacy-aware escalation, and safe boundary handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
